skill.name: pr-review
skill.description: Review MiniMax Skills pull requests by running structural validation first, then applying manual quality guidelines for skill content, scripts, secrets, and repository documentation.
skill.summary: Validate and review MiniMax Skills pull requests against structure and quality standards.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution, weak cryptography, environment access, and path traversal are false positives from Markdown examples, backtick formatting, regex literals, and local status output. The only confirmed risk indicator is an intended standard-library validation script that recursively reads local skill files under a reviewer-selected path; no network access, shell execution, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Intentional Recursive Local File Reading
low_findings.description: The validator accepts a local path, discovers skill directories, and reads supported text files for frontmatter and secret checks. This behavior is expected for a repository validation tool and stays local, but users should run it only on repositories they intend to inspect.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file walking and reading behavior is directly visible in the validator code. It is low severity because there is no network transfer, shell invocation, or secret disclosure beyond local console output.

content.value_statement: Skill pull requests need consistent structure, clear metadata, and safe credential handling. This skill combines a local validator with manual review guidance for MiniMax Skills submissions.
actual_capabilities: Runs a local validation script for MiniMax skill directories.
Checks that each skill has SKILL.md frontmatter with required fields.
Verifies that the skill name matches the directory name.
Scans supported text files for high-confidence secret patterns.
Guides manual review of scope, descriptions, scripts, language, and README updates.
Separates blocker errors from non-blocking warnings for reviewer decisions.
limitations: It is tailored to MiniMax Skills repository conventions.
The validator uses a minimal frontmatter parser and ignores nested YAML details.
Secret scanning covers only selected high-confidence patterns.
Final approval still requires human judgment and repository context.

use_cases.title: Maintain Skill Repository Standards
use_cases.description: Run the validator and review checklist before approving community skill submissions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Skill Contribution
use_cases.description: Check structure, metadata, and documentation expectations before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Audit Marketplace Readiness
use_cases.description: Confirm that submitted skills meet baseline safety, quality, and documentation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Run Basic Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pr-review skill to validate the skill directories in this repository. Report errors, warnings, and the files that need changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a New Skill PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pr-review skill to review this pull request. Start with the validation script, then assess scope, description quality, credential handling, scripts, language, and README sync.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate Validation Failures
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pr-review skill to explain the validation failures in this skill submission. Separate blocker errors from warnings and recommend specific fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Marketplace Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pr-review skill to perform a marketplace readiness review. Include structural compliance, content quality, safety concerns, documentation gaps, and a publish recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that adds a new skill with missing metadata.
output_examples.output: The review flags missing recommended metadata as a warning, confirms required fields, and asks the contributor to update README tables before approval.

output_examples.input: Validate a skill directory that has a name mismatch.
output_examples.output: The result identifies the mismatch as a blocker because the frontmatter name must match the directory name exactly.

output_examples.input: Check a skill that uses an external API.
output_examples.output: The review confirms whether credentials are documented as environment variables and flags any hardcoded key examples for removal.

best_practices: Run the validation script before starting manual content review.
Treat ERROR results as blockers and WARN results as review comments.
Check credential handling and README updates for every new skill submission.
anti_patterns: Approving a skill without running the structural validator.
Treating hardcoded credential examples as acceptable documentation.
Reviewing only SKILL.md while ignoring scripts, references, and README updates.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews MiniMax Skills pull requests for structure, metadata, safety basics, and documentation quality.

faq.question: Does it replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It provides validation and review guidance, but maintainers still make final approval decisions.

faq.question: Can it find every secret?
faq.answer: No. The validator checks selected high-confidence patterns and reviewers must inspect other credential risks manually.

faq.question: Does it run network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The included script uses local filesystem checks and standard Python libraries.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should block a pull request?
faq.answer: Validation errors, missing required fields, directory name mismatches, and confirmed hardcoded secrets should block approval.
